location.office_time_required: in-office (company describes itself as an in-office company)

secondary_archetype: AI Product Manager / Client-Facing Solutions Owner

level_signals: 6+ years of experience owning ambiguous, high-stakes outcomes
Senior individual contributor role explicitly stated
Directs forward deployed engineers and partner teams but is not a people manager
Counterpart is C-suite of Fortune 500 companies

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience owning ambiguous, high-stakes outcomes and earning the trust of senior executives. This includes engagement managers, project leaders, and senior managers from top consulting firms, as well as product managers, founders, and forward-deployed or solutions roles at high-growth technology companies.
A builder's instinct. You want to design and build the actual solution, not just diagnose the problem or manage someone else who builds it.
Strong technical acumen. You can understand and shape AI agent designs and hold your own in a technical conversation with both engineers and executives.
Executive presence and a track record of becoming a trusted advisor to senior decision-makers at large, complex organizations.
Comfort operating in fast-moving, ambiguous environments where you shape the solution as much as you implement it.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A Computer Science, Engineering, or Math degree, or equivalent hands-on technical experience.
An MBA, or equivalent experience at the intersection of business strategy and technology, is a plus.
Experience selling, deploying, or advising on enterprise software or transformation programs at the Fortune 500 level.

scope.surface: Enterprise AI agent deployments for Fortune 500 clients across voice, chat, email, SMS, and other channels
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS / conversational AI platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Successful delivery and adoption of AI agents
core_responsibilities: Own the technical win on Decagon's largest strategic opportunities, partnering with Sales, Solutions Engineers and Forward-deployed engineers to build working AI agents that turn sophisticated, skeptical buyers into signed customers.

Serve as the executive partner to the C-suite of Fortune 500 companies, defining and driving their AI transformation roadmap and owning the relationship from first conversation through production and expansion.
Design, build, and optimize enterprise-grade AI agents in production, going deep on each customer's workflows, pain points, and goals.
Turn the frameworks and playbooks you develop into reusable assets that scale across accounts and feed directly into Decagon's product roadmap.
Direct Forward-deployed engineers and partner teams to deliver at scale while you stay accountable for the outcome.
Run tight feedback loops into Product and Engineering, shaping the product based on what you learn on the front lines of our most demanding deployments.

green_flags: Clear articulation of role ownership and scope — not account management or standardized implementation
Role explicitly described as high-leverage: 'The work compounds in a way most roles don't'
Backed by prominent investors (a16z, Accel, Bain Capital Ventures, Coatue, Index Ventures)
Client list includes recognizable brand names (Cash App, Chime, Oura Health)
Comprehensive benefits package including parental leave, fertility benefits, retirement plan
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned — unclear for Sydney-based international candidates
Role title says 'Product Manager' but responsibilities are closer to a hybrid of solutions engineering, consulting, and technical account ownership — role archetype may be misrepresented
In-office requirement with no remote option stated
Very high expectations (C-suite partner + builder + technical architect + account owner) for a single IC role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 6+ years, 'senior individual contributor' label, and C-suite counterpart signal)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)

location.work_auth and visa_sponsorship (not mentioned; marked unclear)
archetype (role blends consulting, solutions, and PM — classified as Technical Program Manager with secondary AI PM label)
ambiguities: Role is titled 'Product Manager' but the responsibilities (building AI agents for clients, owning customer relationships, pre-sales involvement) are more consistent with a Forward-Deployed Engineer, Solutions Architect, or Technical Account Executive. Archetype set to Technical Program Manager with low-to-medium confidence.
Benefits mention 401K and pension which are typically US/UK instruments; role is listed as Sydney-based, suggesting these may be US headquarters benefits listed generically.
No explicit statement about visa sponsorship or work authorization requirements for Sydney location.
